$comment: THE UX-PARITY GATE CONTRACT. A Godot screen that replaces a React screen must show the SAME
INFORMATION IN THE SAME WORDS (migration decision: option A — faithful reproduction).
Each entry names the React panel being replaced and lists the i18n keys that panel RENDERS.
The gate resolves each key against godot/data/i18n-ja.json (the same ja.ts React reads), then
asserts the text actually appears in the built Godot screen. Interpolated keys ({gold}) are
requiredKeys were derived by reading each React panel's t("...") call sites — they are not a
wish list, they are what the shipped React screen puts on screen.
`evidence` is the comparison screenshot the milestone cannot be declared done without.
AGENTS.md is the authority this encodes: 'Do not call town services done if they are just
lists. Shops must show who can use equipment and what changes. Recovery must show cost,

`fixture` patches the state before the screen builds, so CONDITIONAL and FAILURE surfaces (wounded party, unaffordable, loot to appraise, quest ready to claim, known bestiary entry) are actually asserted — not just the empty happy path.
`worldId` drives a screen from ANOTHER world's pack — the S5 criterion that the same scene code renders both scenarios from data alone, not just the world it was written against.

`exemptScenes` declares the scenes that are NOT player-facing (boot is a one-frame transition). Every other scene under res://scenes/ must appear in `screens` — an unmeasured screen fails the gate, because 'nobody checked it' is how the guild screen escaped for three milestones.

`derivedExclusions` narrows a template family the code cannot actually reach (t(`ns.${k}`) expands to the whole namespace). Every exclusion carries a REASON — a narrowing must stay visible, never silent.

screens.notes: The two-level hub: the square carries the status ledger (first departure vs what came back), the earned checkpoints, and the destinations; each destination holds its services one step in, with Cancel resolving exactly one level. Driven at the square, in all three destinations, with an empty roster, after a wounded return, and with a rest point already reached.

derivedExclusions.reason: React renders this only as the TITLE (hover tooltip) of the disabled 商店 button, never as visible copy. A controller player has no pointer; the port disables the button the same way, and both shipped worlds stock a shop.

screens.notes: Per-wounded row must show hp/maxHp -> maxHp (before/after) and that member's cost; healthy members are NOT listed. town.cannotAffordRecovery must appear when the purse is short.

AGENTS.md: a shop MUST show who can use the equipment (town.canEquip/cannotEquip against the selected adventurer) and what changes (town.equipmentSummary stat deltas). A name+price list is a gate failure.

Appraisal is PAID (loot.appraiseCost badge; loot.appraiseCantAfford when broke). The entry stage previews what would convert (loot.convertible) and offers sell/dismantle — it must NEVER convert on one press. An appraised piece shows a fit comparison vs the chosen adventurer and an equip action.
The CONFIRM stage. Bulk conversion is two-step: the player sees exactly what will be destroyed and what it yields, then answers 実行/やめる. Cancel returns to the entry stage, not out of the service.
Must name the stat each reinforce boosts (workshop.boosts), the materials cost, and the at-cap / cant-afford states (workshop.atCap, workshop.cantAfford).

derivedExclusions.reason: WorkshopPanel reaches career.stat.* only through PRIMARY_STAT_KEY (weapon->attack, accessory->accuracy, else armor); the other stats are not reachable from this screen.
WorkshopPanel reaches career.stat.* only through PRIMARY_STAT_KEY (weapon->attack, accessory->accuracy, else armor); the other stats are not reachable from this screen.
WorkshopPanel reaches career.stat.* only through PRIMARY_STAT_KEY (weapon->attack, accessory->accuracy, else armor); the other stats are not reachable from this screen.
WorkshopPanel reaches career.stat.* only through PRIMARY_STAT_KEY (weapon->attack, accessory->accuracy, else armor); the other stats are not reachable from this screen.
WorkshopPanel reaches career.stat.* only through PRIMARY_STAT_KEY (weapon->attack, accessory->accuracy, else armor); the other stats are not reachable from this screen.

screens.notes: Each quest must show its objective PROGRESS (questBoard.progress) and reward breakdown (rewardGold/rewardXp), plus repeatable / claimedTimes state. An EMPTY board is a real state of this counter (a world may ship no quests, and none are posted before any are authored), so it is driven rather than assumed.

derivedExclusions.reason: Unreachable: the town DISABLES 依頼掲示板 when a world ships no quests (TownEntryPanel), so an empty board cannot be opened, and both shipped worlds author quests. The guard stays in the panel; restore this requirement if a quest-less world ever ships with the counter reachable.

screens.notes: Two-pane: pinned selected adventurer (career.overview/current/mastery) beside the scrolling destination list grouped advanced|foundations. EVERY destination renders its signature, stat shifts, equip slots and granted techniques — this was the exact remediation that fixed 'too thin to decide in'. Driven across a member with NOTHING learned yet and one holding a technique that is not in the combat set, so both the empty case and the 'set this' case are proven.

derivedExclusions.reason: Unreachable with authored content: the game ships FOUR techniques (heal / firebolt / sleep / power-strike) against a loadout limit of 6, so a set can never fill. Restore this requirement the moment a fifth-plus technique or a smaller limit is authored.

No authored vocation shifts damageMin (engine data: every statModifiers block covers maxHp/maxMp/attack/accuracy/armor/speed only). The row renders whatever the data carries; there is nothing to carry.
Same as career.stat.damageMin — no authored vocation shifts it.

screens.notes: The bestiary is the decision surface for preparation: threat, weaknesses and drops per known enemy, not a name list. Three states: a creature KNOWN (weaknesses and drops recorded), one only MET (the entry says what defeating it would reveal), and an empty bestiary before the first fight.

The party menu across its four pages and every state a decision needs: the derived combat numbers and resistances, an afflicted party, the equipment slots, an item that heals / restores 気力 / cures, a piece this adventurer cannot wear, the discard CONFIRM stage, a protected valuable, both empty lists, and the dungeon context where equipment can be read but not changed.

derivedExclusions.reason: React shows an item's EFFECT line only when the catalog has no description for it (localizedCatalogDescription ?? describeConsumable). Every authored item in both worlds ships a ja description, so this fallback cannot render. The port keeps the same fallback.
Same fallback as partyMenu.restoreHp — unreachable while every authored item carries a description.
Same fallback as partyMenu.restoreHp — unreachable while every authored cure carries a description.
The last-resort line for an item with neither description nor effects; no authored item is missing both.
React's aria-label for the tab strip, which is visible and labelled by its own four tabs. Godot has no accessibility tree; a fifth label naming the strip would be noise.

screens.notes: The contextual dock: 探索/耳をすます always, the stair only where one is (and a DISABLED 階段封鎖 with its clue where the way down will not open yet), the way home as stairs or the planted marker, a charm when one is carried, and the auto-explore STATUS at the end. Each is driven, because 'contextual' means the screen is different in each place.

derivedExclusions.reason: React uses this only as the aria-label of the 3D view element (DungeonCockpit). Godot has no accessibility tree, and painting '一人称迷宮ビュー' onto the play screen would be implementation wording of the kind AGENTS.md bars from normal play. The view itself is ported; the label has no player-facing home.

The generic line is React's FALLBACK for a gate with no authored clue (stairGateClue ?? t(...)); all 14 authored gates ship a ja clue, so the fallback cannot render. The port keeps the same fallback — restore this requirement if a clue-less gate is ever authored.

screens.notes: A chest HOLDS the cell (Wizardry prompt model): while it is here the panel owns the command region. It shows only what the party can SEE — closed, a trap found, 'cannot tell', no trap, opened — never a success rate, and 罠を外す appears ONLY once an investigation detected a trap. Every stage is driven, because the whole point of the panel is that the stages read differently.

The party's RECORD of the floor, not the floor's truth: visited cells with their walls, the mark of what is on each (stair, rest, spinner, teleporter, blade floor, gathering point, event, cleared trap, treasure), a compass, and a LEGEND naming every mark — React names them in aria-labels, which a Godot screen has no equivalent for. Driven in the open floor and inside a dark zone.

derivedExclusions.reason: React's aria-label for the minimap grid, beside the visible 周辺 heading it already carries. Godot has no accessibility tree; painting a second name on the same widget would be noise, not information.
React's defensive branch for state.map.floorId being null — reachable only in TOWN, where the map surface is not rendered at all. The port keeps the same fallback.
Same defensive branch: the cell readout says 街 when the party is in no room, which cannot happen while the dungeon map is open.

screens.notes: Orders are given ONE adventurer at a time, front row first. A choice needing a target opens a second STAGE (標的を選ぶ), never a free-aim cursor. Esc backs out exactly one stage. A technique the actor cannot pay for is shown WITH its cost and disabled, not hidden. PARTY PRESENCE: the adventurers are the concept players bring — the party strip must show them at a readable size with portrait, row, HP/MP and status pips (CombatPartyStrip / CharacterPresence), not as thumbnails in a corner.

TARGET SELECTION BELONGS ON THE STAGE, NOT IN A LIST. React deliberately moved it there (CombatCommandMenu.onTargetPreview -> a pulsing reticle follows the cursor on the creature via CombatCockpit/setSelectedTargetId); the list was kept only for the xN count. The Godot port regressed to a plain list and must be rebuilt to drive a stage reticle. (Same panel as combat-command, so the same contract — this entry exists to hold the target-stage screenshot.)
Each scenario is its own WORLD — every card carries that world's authored title and tagline, so the list reads as places rather than a config dropdown.
Only play-affecting toggles, each stating what it DOES to play. AI provider settings and arbitrary save/load are developer tooling and must not appear in normal play (AGENTS.md).
全員でかかる / リピート / オート sit beside the per-actor menu. リピート is unavailable until a round has been declared and SAYS so; オート stops itself when the fight ends or the party is in danger.

The party menu across its four pages and every state a decision needs: the derived combat numbers and resistances, an afflicted party, the equipment slots, an item that heals / restores 気力 / cures, a piece this adventurer cannot wear, the discard CONFIRM stage, a protected valuable, both empty lists, and the dungeon context where equipment can be read but not changed.

derivedExclusions.reason: React shows an item's EFFECT line only when the catalog has no description for it (localizedCatalogDescription ?? describeConsumable). Every authored item in both worlds ships a ja description, so this fallback cannot render. The port keeps the same fallback.
Same fallback as partyMenu.restoreHp — unreachable while every authored item carries a description.
Same fallback as partyMenu.restoreHp — unreachable while every authored cure carries a description.
The last-resort line for an item with neither description nor effects; no authored item is missing both.
React's aria-label for the tab strip, which is visible and labelled by its own four tabs. Godot has no accessibility tree; a fifth label naming the strip would be noise.

screens.notes: The two-level hub: the square carries the status ledger (first departure vs what came back), the earned checkpoints, and the destinations; each destination holds its services one step in, with Cancel resolving exactly one level. Driven at the square, in all three destinations, with an empty roster, after a wounded return, and with a rest point already reached.

derivedExclusions.reason: React renders this only as the TITLE (hover tooltip) of the disabled 商店 button, never as visible copy. A controller player has no pointer; the port disables the button the same way, and both shipped worlds stock a shop.

screens.notes: The guild hall IS the briefing step: the Guild Master, his question, the party you already have, and the ways on. Registration is STAGED from here (AGENTS.md: one decision at a time, visible preview, stable next/back) — never one screen showing every choice at once.
Bounded class list beside a STABLE detail pane. Starting gear is named from the catalog — a raw id ('militia-sabre') in normal play is a gate failure.
Origin (来歴) and impression. The portrait area is RESERVED so the pane does not reflow when one is chosen.
Spend the bonus pool: base vs allocated shown side by side with the remaining points, so the player sees what each point BUYS before committing.

Name, title and notes — with the suggestion line, because a player who does not want to invent one must not be blocked. Text entry is allowed HERE (AGENTS.md permits it where the fantasy requires it).
The expedition result: what was defeated, the xp and gold taken, any level-ups, then the way on. GROWTH is conditional in React, so both the levelled and the un-levelled result are driven.

New run, continue (one row per non-empty save slot, described by what it holds), config. React renders the settings INSIDE this screen, and reports a save it cannot read — both are states of the title, so both are driven here.
